skill.name: smart-journal-monitor
skill.description: Monitor academic journals for breakthrough research articles using configurable keyword and scoring filters.
skill.summary: AI-powered journal monitoring with breakthrough article detection and customizable impact scoring
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 45 static analysis findings are false positives. The analyzer misinterpreted markdown code formatting backticks as Ruby shell execution commands, security checklist items about path traversal as actual traversal threats, documentation text as system reconnaissance, and ArgumentParser as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The skill is a legitimate academic journal monitoring tool. No actual security vulnerabilities or malicious patterns were confirmed after AI review.

content.user_title: Monitor Academic Journals for Breakthrough Research
content.value_statement: Keeping up with thousands of academic papers across top journals is time-consuming and easy to miss important findings. This skill helps you define research keywords, monitor journals like Nature and Science, and receive curated article digests with impact scoring.
actual_capabilities: Identify breakthrough articles using configurable keyword and journal-based scoring
Score articles by journal impact factor and keyword significance
Filter and rank articles by configurable breakthrough threshold
Generate structured research digests with impact scores and takeaways
Monitor multiple academic journals simultaneously with custom keywords
limitations: Does not provide real article data - requires user-provided article feeds or integration
Article scoring uses simplified metadata (journal, title keywords, citation count) not full-text analysis
Does not replace systematic literature review methodologies

use_cases.title: Stay Current with Field Developments
use_cases.description: Define keywords for your domain and receive daily digests of relevant breakthrough papers from top journals.

use_cases.title: Find High-Impact Papers Efficiently
use_cases.description: Quickly filter through large volumes of publications to identify landmark studies in your field.

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence in R&D
use_cases.description: Monitor competitor research output and emerging technologies relevant to your industry.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Journal Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Monitor top journals for new articles about [your research topic]. Focus on high-impact journals and flag any breakthrough findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick scan for recent developments in a specific area

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Research Digest
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a daily digest of breakthrough articles from journals including Nature, Science, and Cell related to [specific research area]. For each article provide a relevance score, key takeaway, and impact assessment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Daily literature review for ongoing research

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Landscape Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Monitor journals for articles from [competitor organization name] or about [competing technology]. Rank findings by novelty and potential market impact.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Topic Research Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up monitoring for [topic 1], [topic 2], and [topic 3] across journals [list journals]. Score each article by cross-topic relevance and flag any interdisciplinary breakthroughs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cross-disciplinary research tracking across multiple fields

output_examples.input: Keywords: immunotherapy, checkpoint inhibitor, Journals: Nature, Science, Cell, NEJM, Lancet
output_examples.output: Three breakthrough articles identified: 1) Novel checkpoint inhibitor targeting LAG-3 shows promise in melanoma (Nature, score 85) - First-in-class mechanism with strong early efficacy data. 2) Combined CAR-T and checkpoint blockade strategy for solid tumors (Science, score 70) - Novel combination approach addressing key limitation. 3) Biomarker discovery for immunotherapy response prediction (Cell, score 65) - Enables patient stratification.

output_examples.input: Monitor for CRISPR gene editing advances in top journals this month
output_examples.output: Two high-scoring articles found: Prime editing efficiency breakthrough in non-dividing cells with novel delivery method (Nature Biotechnology, score 90). New base editor variant reduces off-target effects by 10x (Science, score 75). One medium-priority article on CRISPR diagnostics application.

best_practices: Define specific, targeted keywords rather than broad terms to get the most relevant results
Adjust the breakthrough threshold based on your field - some areas have more frequent high-impact publications
Review scored articles critically - automated scoring is a filter, not a final judgment of importance
anti_patterns: Do not rely solely on keyword matching to determine article importance - read full abstracts for nuanced assessment
Avoid monitoring too many journals at once - focus on the most relevant ones for your specific field
Do not bypass manual validation of breakthrough classifications - the scoring is a heuristic, not definitive proof

faq.question: What journals does this skill support?
faq.answer: The default set includes Nature, Science, Cell, NEJM, and Lancet. You can customize the journal list to any academic journals relevant to your field.

faq.question: How are breakthrough scores calculated?
faq.answer: Scores are based on journal impact factor (30 points), presence of breakthrough keywords in titles (20 points), and early citation counts (10 points). Threshold defaults to 40 points.

faq.question: Can I use this for systematic literature reviews?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for awareness and triage of new publications. It does not replace systematic review methodologies which require structured search protocols and exhaustive coverage.

faq.question: Does this skill access live journal databases?
faq.answer: This skill analyzes article metadata you provide. It does not connect to external APIs or databases directly.

faq.question: How often should I run monitoring?
faq.answer: Daily monitoring is recommended for fast-moving fields. Weekly may be sufficient for established research areas with slower publication rates.

faq.question: Can I monitor non-English journals?
faq.answer: The keyword matching works with any language, but the built-in breakthrough keywords (novel, breakthrough, first, landmark) are English-language terms. You may want to add equivalent terms in other languages.
